behaviors.description: SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to correct sum of cells A1 through A10
behaviors.justification: A Unit test of the SUM algorithm alone (mock cell list) would miss failures in range parsing ('A1:A10' not correctly parsed as 10 cells) and cell lookup mechanisms. Real failure: range bounds are off-by-one or cells are looked up incorrectly. Requires in-memory spreadsheet with actual cells to verify range resolution.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory spreadsheet with cells A1 through A10 containing values 1–10. Enter formula =SUM(A1:A10). Verify result equals 55. Specifically test: range bounds parsing (off-by-one errors), cell ID resolution (e.g., A10 vs A1), and correct arithmetic.

behaviors.description: Cell referencing another cell with formula (e.g., B1==A1*2 where A1==B1) produces correct cascaded result
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit test with mocked cell values misses failures in dependency detection and evaluation order. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking cell values hides bugs in tracking which cells reference which, and in topological ordering of recalculation. Real failure: dependencies aren't detected, or evaluation happens in wrong order (infinite loop or stale values). Requires multi-cell spreadsheet with real inter-cell wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Create spreadsheet with A1=10, B1==A1*2 (formula). Verify B1=20. Change A1 to 15. Verify B1 automatically updates to 30 (not stale at 20). Test dependency detection (B1 recognizes dependency on A1), evaluation order, and caching invalidation on source change.

behaviors.description: Engine detects circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns error instead of looping
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: Unit test of cycle-detection algorithm in isolation (mock dependency graph) hides failures in formula parsing or graph construction. The real failure occurs in the full pipeline: parse formula text → build dependency graph → detect cycles. If formula parsing is broken, cycle detection never runs. Requires full spreadsheet environment with real formula entry and parsing.
behaviors.test_description: Create spreadsheet cell A1. Enter formula =B1. Enter formula =A1 in B1. Verify system returns 'circular reference' error (not infinite loop, not silent failure). Test cycle detection across the full pipeline: formula parsing, dependency graph construction, and cycle detection algorithm.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with exact match returns correct value; returns error when key not found
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit test with mocked lookup table (data structure) would test VLOOKUP function logic but miss failures in how the range A1:D10 is resolved from cells. Real failure: lookup table range isn't identified correctly, or wrong cells are included. Requires cells forming the actual lookup table.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory spreadsheet with lookup table (A1:B5: keys in col A, values in col B). Enter formula =VLOOKUP(lookup_value, A1:B5, 2, FALSE). Test with matching key (verify correct value returned) and non-matching key (verify #N/A error). Test range resolution, value matching logic, and error handling.

behaviors.description: IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low"))
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit test of IF logic in isolation misses failures in formula parsing (nested structure not understood) or cell reference evaluation. The behavior involves a formula with cell references, requiring the full pipeline: parse nested formula → evaluate conditions by looking up A1 → return correct branch. Real failure is in formula parser or cell resolution, not IF logic alone.
behaviors.test_description: Create spreadsheet with A1=25. Enter formula =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low") in B1. Verify B1="medium". Test with A1=5 (expect "low") and A1=30 (expect "high"). Test formula parsing of nesting, condition evaluation with cell lookup, and branch selection.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with correct separator and skips empty cells (not inserting blank strings)
behaviors.justification: CONCATENATE function logic (handling empty cells) can be tested in isolation. Real failure is in the function algorithm: incorrectly treating empty values as blank strings or inserting separators for empty cells. This failure is independent of how cells are represented in the spreadsheet—only the function's handling of empty inputs matters. Unit test with correct representation of empty values (None, null, or sentinel) is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Call CONCATENATE function directly with list of values including empty: CONCATENATE([5, EMPTY_VALUE, 10], separator=","). Verify result is "5,10" (empty skipped, no extra separator). Test function logic for empty-cell handling without spreadsheet infrastructure. Verify no leading/trailing separators.

behaviors.description: Formula referencing cell in different sheet (e.g., =Sheet2!A1+1) resolves correctly when referenced sheet exists
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit test with mocked sheet lookup misses failures in workbook structure or sheet identification. Real failure: sheet name parsing fails, sheet isn't found in workbook, or wrong sheet is accessed. Requires multi-sheet workbook structure with actual sheets to verify cross-sheet reference resolution.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2. In Sheet2, set A1=10. In Sheet1, enter formula =Sheet2!A1+1 in cell B1. Verify B1=11. Test sheet name parsing (Sheet2 correctly identified), sheet lookup in workbook, and value resolution across sheets.

behaviors.description: When referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas recalculate in correct topological order
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the dependency graph hides failures in building it from formulas or tracking dependent cells. Real failure: when A1 changes, B1 (depends on A1) updates, but C1 (depends on B1) doesn't recalculate, or recalculates before B1 (wrong order). Requires multi-cell spreadsheet with real dependency chains and change propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Create spreadsheet: A1=1, B1==A1*2, C1==B1+10. Verify B1=2, C1=12. Change A1 to 5. Verify B1=10 and C1=20 (both recalculated, not stale). Test dependency graph construction, topological sort order, and change propagation through multiple levels without cycles or missed cells.

behaviors.description: Undo operation after formula edit restores formula text and all previously computed dependent values
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: Unit test of undo-stack algorithm misses failures in applying undo to actual spreadsheet state or recalculating dependents after restoration. Real failure: undo stack updated but formulas/values not restored, or dependent cells not recalculated. Requires multi-cell spreadsheet with state persistence and undo/redo mechanism.
behaviors.test_description: Create spreadsheet with B1==A1*2. Set A1=10 (B1=20). Change A1 to 20 (B1=40). Undo. Verify A1=10, B1=20, and B1's formula text unchanged. Test undo stack maintenance, state restoration to prior values/formulas, and dependent recalculation after undo.

behaviors.description: Saving workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas—no data loss or precision change in floating-point results
behaviors.justification: Local file I/O is Integration-level infrastructure. Real failures: floating-point precision loss during serialization, formula text corruption, incorrect encoding/decoding of special values. Mock file I/O could hide precision issues if not carefully simulated, but real local file write/read is Integration (not System, since System is for subprocess or OS-level behavior beyond local FS).
behaviors.test_description: Create spreadsheet with A1=π (floating-point), B1==A1*2, C1="text". Save to temporary file in custom binary format. Load workbook back. Verify A1 has identical bit-for-bit precision (no rounding), B1 recalculates to 2π, C1="text", and formula text in B1 is unchanged. Test serialization encoding, precision preservation, and deserialization integrity.
